title: Fixed Income Foundations
tagline: Learn how the world's biggest market actually works — bonds, yields, duration, and credit.
description: The bond market is larger than the stock market, and every corner of finance is priced off it. This path takes you from what a bond literally is to the two risk numbers professionals live by — duration and convexity — and the spread that prices credit risk. No prior fixed-income knowledge assumed; you should be comfortable with the idea of present value.
outcome: You can explain why bond prices fall when rates rise, why duration is not maturity, what a yield quote actually assumes, and how to tell a rate loss from a credit loss — the core technical vocabulary of any fixed-income conversation, interview or desk.
audience: Students preparing for finance recruiting and early-career professionals who need bond mechanics for real.
